from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Predicates are turned into readable phrases; triples that share the
    same subject are merged into one sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language phrases.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the object value.
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": "has the alternative name {obj}",
        "bodyStyle": "has body style {obj}",
        "engine": "is powered by {obj}",
        "manufacturer": "was manufactured by {obj}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "is related to {obj}",
        "transmission": "has transmission {obj}",
        "wheelbase": "has a wheelbase of {obj}",
        "builder": "was built by {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed on {obj}",
        "length": "is {obj} long",
        "powerType": "uses power type {obj}",
        "shipClass": "belongs to the {obj} class",
        "shipDisplacement": "displaces {obj} tonnes",
        "shipLaunch": "was launched on {obj}",
        "shipOrdered": "was ordered on {obj}",
        "shipPower": "has ship power {obj}",
        "topSpeed": "has a top speed of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "christeningDate": "was christened on {obj}",
        "maidenVoyage": "made its maiden voyage on {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "shipBeam": "has a beam of {obj} metres",
        "shipInService": "has been in service since {obj}",
        "status": "has status {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "has been active since {obj}",
        "shipLaidDown": "was laid down on {obj}",
        "buildDate": "was built between {obj}",
        "cylinderCount": "has {obj} cylinders",
        "totalProduction": "was produced in a total of {obj} units",
        "countryOrigin": "originates from {obj}",
        "diameter": "has a diameter of {obj} metres",
        "failedLaunches": "experienced {obj} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": "has {obj} rocket stage(s)",
        "totalLaunches": "has a total of {obj} launch(es)",
        "assembly": "was assembled in {obj}",
        "class": "belongs to the {obj} class",
        "designer": "was designed by {obj}",
        "modelYears": "was produced in {obj}",
        "country": "is a country called {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {obj}",
        "designCompany": "was designed by {obj}",
        "productionStartYear": "started production in {obj}",
        "width": "is {obj} metres wide",
        "layout": "has layout {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "has parent company {obj}",
        "operator": "is operated by {obj}",
        "product": "produces {obj}",
        "city": "is based in the city of {obj}",
        "successor": "was succeeded by {obj}",
        "fate": "met its fate as {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "has subsidiary {obj}",
        "comparable": "is comparable to {obj}",
        "finalFlight": "had its final flight on {obj}",
        "function": "serves as {obj}",
        "launchSite": "has launch site {obj}",
        "maidenFlight": "had its maiden flight on {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "partialFailures": "had {obj} partial failure(s)",
        "site": "is a site called {obj}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in {obj}",
        "associatedRocket": "is associated with {obj}",
        "saint": "has saint {obj}",
        "employer": "is employed by {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "productionEndYear": "ended production in {obj}",
        "division": "has division {obj}",
        "shipDraft": "has a draft of {obj}",
        "modelStartYear": "started model year in {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj} km²",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "extinctionDate": "went extinct on {obj}",
    }

    def predicate_phrase(p: str, obj: str) -> str:
        # Use explicit mapping if available
        if p in phrase_map:
            return phrase_map[p].format(obj=obj)
        # Fallback: split camelCase / PascalCase and prepend a generic verb
        words = re.sub(r'([a-z])([A-Z])', r'\1 \2', p).lower()
        return f"has {words} {obj}"

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove parentheses, extra quotes and surrounding whitespace)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        """
        Clean the object string:
        - Strip surrounding whitespace.
        - Remove surrounding double quotes.
        - Preserve numeric values with their units (e.g., "120.0 (inches)" → "120.0 inches").
        - Remove any remaining parenthetical content.
        - Strip trailing commas, periods and whitespace.
        """
        o = o.strip()
        # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
        if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
            o = o[1:-1]

        # Preserve value‑unit pairs like "120.0 (inches)"
        import re
        m = re.match(r'^(.*?\S)\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*$', o)
        if m:
            value = m.group(1).strip()
            unit = m.group(2).strip()
            o = f"{value} {unit}"
        else:
            # Remove any other parenthetical content
            o = re.sub(r'\s*\([^)]*\)', '', o).strip()

        # Strip trailing commas, periods and whitespace
        o = o.rstrip('.,').strip()
        return o

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        # clean the object before storing
        cleaned = clean_obj(t.object)
        grouped[t.subject].append(Triple(t.subject, t.predicate, cleaned))

    # Build a single complex sentence by chaining subjects with relative clauses
    subject_predicates = []  # list of (subject, predicate_string)
    for subj, items in grouped.items():
        preds = [predicate_phrase(t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
        if len(preds) == 1:
            pred_str = preds[0]
        else:
            # Join multiple predicates with commas and 'and' before the last one
            if len(preds) == 2:
                pred_str = f"{preds[0]} and {preds[1]}"
            else:
                pred_str = f"{', '.join(preds[:-1])}, and {preds[-1]}"
        subject_predicates.append((subj, pred_str))

    if not subject_predicates:
        return ""

    # Start with the first subject and its predicates
    first_subj, first_pred = subject_predicates[0]
    sentence = f"{first_subj} {first_pred}"

    # Chain remaining subjects using relative clauses ("which ...")
    for idx, (subj, pred) in enumerate(subject_predicates[1:], start=1):
        connector = ", which " if idx < len(subject_predicates) - 1 else ", and which "
        # Use only the predicate part after the subject for the relative clause
        sentence += f"{connector}{pred}"

    sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
